title: ComponentMeta<any> in contract-fields type alias
summary: ContractFieldSource uses Partial<Pick<ComponentMeta<any>, ...>>. ComponentMeta<TProps = Record<string, unknown>> has a defaulted type parameter, and the fields being picked (name, description, props, replaces, pixelCorners, shadowSystem, styleOwnership, structuralRules, density, composition, wraps, a11y) do NOT depend on TProps. Using `any` defeats the purpose and triggers no-explicit-any.
proposed_action: Replace `ComponentMeta<any>` with plain `ComponentMeta` (uses the default `Record<string, unknown>` generic). None of the picked fields use TProps, so the parameter is free to use the default.

title: ComponentType<any> in preview ComponentRegistry
summary: `type ComponentRegistry = Record<string, ComponentType<any>>` uses ComponentType<any>. PreviewPage renders registered components with no props (`<Component />`), so an explicit prop shape is unnecessary. React's ComponentType defaults to ComponentType<{}>, but here we store components with varied prop signatures and render them parameter-less.
proposed_action: Replace `ComponentType<any>` with `ComponentType<Record<string, unknown>>`. Matches the default TProps elsewhere in @rdna/preview and allows any component to be stored without resorting to `any`. (Alternative: `ComponentType` without a parameter — but the explicit Record form is more consistent with @rdna/preview's own conventions.)

title: Input.Validity render-prop uses Record<string, unknown> instead of FieldValidityState
summary: InputValidityProps declares `children: (state: { validity: Record<string, unknown> }) => React.ReactNode`. The component delegates to BaseField.Validity, whose children signature is `(state: FieldValidityState) => React.ReactNode` with `validity: FieldValidityData['state']` (the concrete base-ui validity object). `Record<string, unknown>` throws away the known shape.
proposed_action: Import `FieldValidity` namespace or `FieldValidityState` from `@base-ui/react/field` and retype InputValidityProps as `{ children: (state: FieldValidity.State) => React.ReactNode }`. This mirrors the upstream type exactly and removes the weak Record.

title: BlockNote block generator emits `any` for render callbacks
summary: The generator writes `render: (props: any) => ...` and `render: ({ contentRef }: any) => ...` into the auto-generated blocknote-blocks.tsx. The comment claims BlockNote generics are too deep, but the repo already ships a hand-rolled adapter type — `BlockNoteRenderProps` in packages/radiants/blocknote/types.ts — that every render function (e.g. renderAlertBlock, renderCardBlock) already consumes as its parameter type. The `any` just re-widens an already narrow shape.
proposed_action: Update generate-blocknote-blocks.ts to (a) emit `import type { BlockNoteRenderProps } from '../blocknote/types';` into generated/blocknote-blocks.tsx, and (b) replace the three `as any` literals with `BlockNoteRenderProps`. For `{ contentRef }`, the destructured parameter typed as `BlockNoteRenderProps` still works because BlockNoteRenderProps has contentRef. Re-run `pnpm --filter @rdna/radiants generate:blocknote` afterward to refresh the generated file.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Alert Demo coerces variant via `as any`
summary: `<Alert.Root variant={variant as any} ...>` coerces a string-typed Demo prop into Alert's narrow AlertVariant union ('default' | 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'info'). AlertVariant is declared locally in Alert.tsx but is not exported, forcing registry Demo to reach for `any`.
proposed_action: Export `AlertVariant` from packages/radiants/components/core/Alert/Alert.tsx (named type export), import it in runtime-attachments.tsx, and replace `variant as any` with `variant as AlertVariant`. Alternatively use `React.ComponentProps<typeof Alert.Root>['variant']` to avoid a new export.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Drawer Demo coerces direction via `as any`
summary: `<Drawer.Provider ... direction={direction as any}>` — the direction prop is narrowed on Drawer.Provider but the Demo signature treats it as unknown and casts to `any`.
proposed_action: Replace with `direction as React.ComponentProps<typeof Drawer.Provider>['direction']`. This pulls the exact union from Drawer.Provider's prop surface without needing to export a new named type.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — DropdownMenuContent side coerced via `as any`
summary: `{...(position ? { side: position as any } : {})}` — DropdownMenuContent's side prop is a narrow union ('top'|'right'|'bottom'|'left' per base-ui). Demo widens the input to `unknown` then casts to any.
proposed_action: Replace `position as any` with `position as React.ComponentProps<typeof DropdownMenuContent>['side']`. Resolves at compile time; no new exports needed.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Input Demo coerces size via `as any`
summary: `<Input ... {...(size ? { size: size as any } : {})} ...>`. InputSize (`'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'`) is already publicly exported from Input.types.ts.
proposed_action: Replace `size as any` with `size as InputSize`. Import `InputSize` from '../components/core/Input/Input.types' at the top of runtime-attachments.tsx.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Icon Demo `name as any` and `className as any`
summary: Icon's `name` prop is already declared `name: string` and `className?: string`. The Demo coerces via `as any` despite no narrower type on the Icon side.
proposed_action: Replace `name as any` with `name as string` and `className as any` with `className as string`. No upstream changes needed — Icon already accepts these scalar types.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — NavigationMenu.Root orientation via `as any`
summary: `{...(orientation ? { orientation: orientation as any } : {})}` — NavigationMenu.Root accepts 'horizontal' | 'vertical' (base-ui union). Demo widens then casts to any.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Select.Trigger size via `as any`
summary: `<Select.Trigger ... size={size as any} ...>` — Select's size prop follows the same 'sm'|'md'|'lg' convention used across the package.
proposed_action: Replace `size as any` with `size as React.ComponentProps<typeof Select.Trigger>['size']`. Resolves at compile time.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Slider Demo size via `as any`
proposed_action: Replace `size as any` with `size as React.ComponentProps<typeof Slider>['size']`.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Switch Demo size + labelPosition via `as any`
summary: `<Switch ... {...(size ? { size: size as any } : {})} {...(labelPosition ? { labelPosition: labelPosition as any } : {})} ...>`.
proposed_action: Use `React.ComponentProps<typeof Switch>['size']` and `['labelPosition']` respectively. Compile-time lookup, no new exports.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Tabs Demo mode via `as any`
summary: `<Tabs defaultValue="design" mode={mode as any} ...>` — `TabsMode` = `'capsule' | 'chrome'` is already publicly exported from Tabs.tsx.
proposed_action: Import `TabsMode` from '../components/core/Tabs/Tabs' in runtime-attachments.tsx and replace with `mode as TabsMode`.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — ToggleGroup Demo variant via `as any`
proposed_action: Replace `variant as any` with `variant as React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroup>['variant']`.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Tooltip Demo position via `as any`
Vendored Dotting engine: @ts-nocheck on eight files (keep as-is)
summary: Eight files in the vendored Dotting canvas engine start with `// @ts-nocheck`. Four are annotated as `— vendored canvas engine; strict null checks deferred` / `— vendored Dotting component; optional props pass-through`. These are the only @ts-nocheck suppressions in the repo and each file is a non-trivial legacy port. Also contains one `(...args: any[]) => void` type and one `as any // this is to forestall typescript error` — all guarded by the file-level nocheck so they do not leak weak types to consumers.
proposed_action: keep_with_reason. Do NOT remove these suppressions during this cleanup pass — this is a vendored/legacy engine that would need a dedicated typing migration (separate from the cleanup audit). Recommend: (a) add explicit `// eslint-disable-next-line rdna/require-exception-metadata` stubs with owner and an issue reference to track the eventual migration, and (b) confirm these files are not re-exported with looser types; internal usage is already walled off.

title: runtime-attachments.tsx — Breadcrumbs Demo separator cast uses `as string` narrowing — NOT weak
summary: The many `as string`, `as boolean`, `as number`, `as 16 | 24` casts in runtime-attachments.tsx are NOT `as any`. They coerce `Record<string, unknown>` Demo props into concrete scalar types for forwarding. Included here for completeness: reviewers sometimes conflate these with weak typing.
proposed_action: keep_with_reason. These are already the target shape and are only needed because the Demo signature intentionally accepts `Record<string, unknown>` (registry dispatch). A broader cleanup (e.g., typed Demo signatures parameterized by component props) would eliminate them, but is out of scope for this audit and used in fewer than 3 cross-cutting sites.
